name: Utilitarianism
tagline: The right act is whichever produces the most well-being.
summary: Utilitarianism judges actions by consequences: the morally best choice is the one that maximizes aggregate well-being and minimizes suffering. It demands we take seriously the experiences of every being our choices touch.

quotes.text: It is better to be a human dissatisfied than a pig satisfied.
quotes.author: John Stuart Mill

quotes.text: The greatest happiness of the greatest number.
quotes.author: Jeremy Bentham

quotes.text: The question is not, Can they reason? but, Can they suffer?
quotes.author: Jeremy Bentham

quotes.text: If we can prevent suffering, without sacrificing comparable good, we ought to.
quotes.author: Peter Singer
